scope: S4096 compressed shared-KV dKV producer diagnostic for sparse-MLA backward

read.promoted: CK-Tile direct-fragment MFMA producer is a better low-level owner than the old rocWMMA store_matrix_sync/shared-tile route-grid body. It cuts the diagnostic raw-atomic dKV producer floor from about 8.43 ms to 5.62 ms at token_block=8 and remains 6.35 ms at token_block=16.
read.rejected: The dense active-window grouped epilogue is not viable even though it models 11.9x to 23.8x fewer global writes. Initializing, updating, and flushing the 688xD16 shared table makes it 11.14-12.08 ms, slower than raw CK atomics and roughly at the old full sparse-attention boundary.
read.not_e2e_candidate: These probes still consume diagnostic materialized probabilities and scaled dP. They are not integrated TorchTitan sparse-attention kernels and therefore do not get an e2e TFLOPs promotion.
read.next_kernel_contract: Build the cooperative HIP/CK sparse-attention backward owner around the CK direct-fragment producer, but replace the dense shared table with a sparse segmented epilogue over active route lanes/slots: q,dO,kv,indices,lse/sink -> recompute score/prob/scaled-dP -> CK MFMA contribution fragment -> lane/warp/block segmented accumulation for repeated compressed groups -> reduced dKV writes, all before any HBM score/prob/partial slab.
